title: WOS API Task
description: Public API resource schema for WOS tasks. Greenfield per ADR 0082 D-15 step 4. A `Task` is a single unit of governed actor work created by a kernel `createTask` action (Kernel S9.2) and governed by the Workflow Governance task-management contract (governance S10). The wire shape carries identity, workflow binding, assignment, contract context, lifecycle (closed `TaskStatus`), and the optional draft response. The lighter `TaskListItem` shape is used for list views; the full `Task` is the single-resource shape. Submission is split into a draft (idempotent partial save) and a final response (require `Idempotency-Key`). The kernel runtime artifact `wos-process.schema.json#/$defs/ActiveTask` is prior art; this projection is greenfield (ADR 0082 D-15).

ActorRef.description: Stable actor reference from the identity/governance subsystem (ADR 0082 D-9). Hoisted to `_common.schema.json` per PLN-0404.

WosResourceUrn.description: Public WOS API resource URN (ADR 0082 D-4). Hoisted to `_common.schema.json` per PLN-0404.

WorkflowUrl.description: Absolute URI of the governing WOS Workflow Document.

TaskStatus.description: Closed user-facing task lifecycle for the public API (ADR 0082 D-12). The kernel runtime carries a richer `created|assigned|claimed|delegated|escalated` set on `wos-process.schema.json#/$defs/ActiveTask.status` for processor-side bookkeeping; the public API collapses those into the user-visible posture: `pending` (the actor has work to do); `drafted` (a partial response is saved); `submitted` (the actor's final response is committed and the task is no longer actionable); `dismissed` (the actor declined the task per delegation or escalation policy); `expired` (the SLA `targetDuration` elapsed without submission and the breach policy fired). No vendor-extension seam: the user-facing taxonomy is closed by design; richer kernel-side states surface in provenance and on `TaskOutcomeEvent.outcome`.

TaskKind.description: Closed-with-vendor-extension task family (ADR 0082 D-12). Reserved literals: `intake` (initial application capture); `review` (governance review-protocol step); `determination` (rights-impacting decision per Kernel S9.2 / governance S6); `signature` (signature-profile signer step); `correspondence-response` (reply to inbound correspondence); `hold-resolution` (action that resumes a typed hold per governance S12); `escalation` (manual escalation step); `verification` (independent confirmation of a prior mutation, see `verificationLevel` in `instance.schema.json`). Vendor extensions MUST use an `x-` prefix.

TaskBinding.description: Closed contract-binding kind for the task body (Kernel S7.2). Byte-aligned mirror of the kernel `ActiveTask.binding` enum at `wos-process.schema.json#/$defs/ActiveTask/properties/binding` (ADR 0082 D-13 gate 6). `formspec` is rendered by Formspec; `jsonSchema` is a generic JSON-Schema-validated payload; `freeform` is a structured task with no machine-validated payload (typical of dismissals or simple acknowledgements). Closed-no-extension at this layer; if a deployment needs new bindings, the kernel grows them and the API mirror tracks.

ReviewProtocolKind.description: Closed-with-vendor-extension review-protocol classification (governance §4.1 Protocol Definitions, §4.2 Protocol Combination; workflow-governance.md:248-260). Reserved literals: `independentFirst` (the reviewer's independent assessment MUST be recorded before any agent/upstream recommendation is accessible — §4.2 enforcement obligation); `considerOpposite` (the reviewer is required to consider the opposite outcome before settling); `calibratedConfidence` (the reviewer's confidence is calibrated against historical accuracy); `dualBlind` (two independent reviewers without cross-visibility); `unassisted` (the reviewer is not exposed to upstream recommendations). Vendor extensions MUST use an `^x-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$` prefix per ADR 0082 D-12.

TaskDeadline.description: Single named SLA window on a task (governance §10.4.1 multi-SLA authoring; workflow-governance.md:548-560 — `slaDefinitions: array<SlaDefinition>`). The current single-valued `deadline` field captures only one of `firstResponse` / `fullResolution` / statutory windows; this typed sub-shape exposes the full set so the applicant sees both clocks and the staff portal prioritizes by which window is closer to breach.

kind.description: Closed-no-extension deadline classification. `sla` is a service-level agreement window; `statutory` is a legally-binding regulatory window (typically `enforcing` severity); `internal` is an organizational target (typically `advisory`). Closed by design — adding a new kind requires a schema major bump.

at.description: UTC RFC 3339 absolute deadline timestamp, or the literal `never` (ADR 0082 D-10 sentinel pattern). The window expires at this instant; warning notifications fire at workflow-declared lead-time before.

severity.description: Closed-no-extension severity classification. `advisory` deadlines surface to UI but do not by themselves trigger escalation; `enforcing` deadlines fire the workflow's breach-policy escalation chain on expiry (governance §10.3 + §10.4 escalation chains).

id.description: Optional stable identifier matching the workflow's `slaDefinitions[].id` (governance §10.4.1). Lets clients correlate a deadline with the named window it implements (`firstResponse`, `fullResolution`).

AssignmentRoles.description: Five-role task-assignment projection (governance §7.2 separation of duties + §10.2 assignment roles; workflow-governance.md:392-396 + 502-514). Surfaces the kernel's task-management role taxonomy so a staff portal can answer "who is excluded?", "who is the required owner?", "which actor pool may claim?", "who currently holds the task?", and "who delegated it?" without re-deriving from delegation graphs and provenance. Closed object — adding a new role requires a schema major bump; the precedence rule from workflow-governance.md:514 (`excludedOwner` overrides all other roles) is enforced at the runtime, not in the projection.

excludedOwner.description: Actor URN explicitly excluded from assignment to enforce separation of duties (governance §7.2; workflow-governance.md:512). Per workflow-governance.md:514 this overrides all other roles — an actor appearing here MUST NOT be assigned even if otherwise eligible. Absent when no exclusion applies.

requiredOwner.description: Actor URN required to be the owner of this task. Maps to the kernel `owner` role (workflow-governance.md:508) when the workflow declaration pins a specific actor; absent when the task uses a pool-based assignment instead.

eligiblePool.description: Actor URNs in the eligible pool — first-claim-wins per workflow-governance.md:510 (`potentialOwner` role). Empty when the task uses single-actor assignment.

currentAssignee.description: Actor URN currently responsible for the task (`Task.assignedActor` mirror — projected here for shape symmetry). Updated on delegation or escalation.

delegatedFrom.description: Actor URN that delegated this task to the current `currentAssignee`. Mirrors `Task.delegatedFrom`; surfaced under `assignmentRoles` so consumers reading the role projection do not need to cross-reference the parent task's top-level fields.

businessAdministrator.description: Actor URN who can reassign, delegate, or escalate this task without performing it (workflow-governance.md:511). Typically a supervisor or governance officer. Absent when the workflow does not declare a business administrator on this task pattern.

ImpactLevel.description: Closed kernel impact level (Kernel §6 proportionality index). Byte-aligned mirror of `wos-workflow.schema.json#/$defs/ImpactLevel` (ADR 0082 D-13 gate 6). Defaults to the workflow's declared `impactLevel` when not overridden on the task pattern. Closed-no-extension by design: the four-literal taxonomy is normative across kernel, governance, AI-oversight, and review-protocol gating.

ResponseValue.description: Response payload submitted by the actor, carried as an opaque JSON value (typically an object). The structural contract for this value is the workflow's task contract (Kernel S7.2 + governance S10), resolved via the task's `contractRef`, `definitionUrl`, and `definitionVersion`. The reference server validates the payload against the resolved contract at submission time; this contract treats the value as opaque-shaped. The wire posture matches `CaseStateMutation.previousValue` / `newValue` and `EvaluationResult.mutations[].newValue` — any JSON value, validated downstream against the workflow.

^x-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$.$comment: Vendor-extension fields for deployment-local validation metadata.

ValidationOutcome.description: Most recent WOS validation wrapper for a Formspec-backed task response (Kernel §13.6; wos-process.schema.json#/$defs/ValidationOutcome). The three booleans are the validation axes: Formspec response envelope validity, task-pin match, and Formspec definition validation over response.data.

envelopeValid.description: Whether the response envelope validated against Formspec response.schema.json.

pinMatch.description: Whether response.definitionUrl and response.definitionVersion matched the task's pinned contract reference.

definitionValid.description: Whether Formspec Definition validation over response.data passed.

errors.description: WOS-level validation errors, including envelope and pin errors. Empty when all three validation axes pass.

items.description: Single WOS validation error. Carries a human-readable message plus optional machine-readable code and location.

code.description: Optional deployment or validator error code.

path.description: Optional JSON Pointer or form path naming the failing response location.

validationResults.description: Formspec-shaped ValidationResult entries from Definition validation. Omitted when envelope validation or pin validation prevents definition validation from running.

items.description: Single Formspec validation result projected through the WOS wrapper.

path.description: Optional Formspec field path or JSON Pointer associated with the validation result.

TaskListItem.description: Lightweight task projection for list views (ADR 0082 D-3). Carries identity, classification, status, deadline, and binding metadata — enough to drive an inbox-style list without forcing the heavier `Task` body. The full `Task` shape is fetched per row on detail navigation.

id.description: Stable public URN for this task.

instanceId.description: Case instance URN this task belongs to.

workflowUrl.description: Absolute URI of the governing Workflow Document.

assignedActor.description: Actor URN currently responsible for this task. Updated on delegation or escalation.

deadline.description: UTC RFC 3339 deadline by which the actor SHOULD submit the response before SLA breach (governance S10.3).

createdAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the task was created.

updatedAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the task projection was last refreshed.

Task.description: Full public task projection (ADR 0082 D-3). Adds contract binding, draft state, and validation outcome to the `TaskListItem` core. The kernel runtime artifact `wos-process.schema.json#/$defs/ActiveTask` is prior art; this is greenfield (ADR 0082 D-15) so the projection diverges intentionally: identifiers are URNs (D-4), `status` is the closed user-facing taxonomy (not the kernel `created|assigned|claimed|...` set), and the form-rendering `context` block is not surfaced (clients fetch the form via `definitionUrl`/`definitionVersion`).

id.description: Stable public URN for this task.

instanceId.description: Case instance URN this task belongs to.

workflowUrl.description: Absolute URI of the governing Workflow Document.

description.description: Optional longer-form task description for actor context.

assignedActor.description: Actor URN currently responsible for this task. Updated on delegation or escalation.

delegatedFrom.description: Actor URN that delegated this task to the current `assignedActor`. Omitted when the task was directly assigned.

contractRef.description: Key into the Workflow Document's `contracts` map identifying which contract governs this task body (Kernel S7.2). REQUIRED when `binding` is `formspec` or `jsonSchema`.

definitionUrl.description: Form or contract definition URL pinned at task creation. REQUIRED when `binding` is `formspec` or `jsonSchema`. Clients render the body by resolving this URL together with `definitionVersion`.

definitionVersion.description: Form or contract definition version pinned at task creation. REQUIRED when `binding` is `formspec` or `jsonSchema`.

deadlines.description: Full set of named SLA / statutory / internal deadlines on this task (governance §10.4.1; workflow-governance.md:548-560). Carries `firstResponse` and `fullResolution` for multi-SLA workflows plus any statutory windows. Empty when the workflow declares no deadlines on this task pattern.

reviewProtocol.description: Closed-with-vendor-extension review-protocol classification (governance §4.1-§4.2; workflow-governance.md:248-260). Surfaces the `independentFirst` / `considerOpposite` / `calibratedConfidence` / `dualBlind` / `unassisted` posture so a staff portal can lock recommendation visibility until the reviewer's independent assessment is recorded. Present iff `kind == review` and the workflow declares a review protocol on this task pattern.

mustCompleteBeforeContinuationEnds.description: When true, this task MUST be completed before the case's `continuationOfServicesEndsAt` window closes (governance §3.6 cross-cut; workflow-governance.md:154-158). Applicant- and staff-facing UIs surface this flag to prioritize work that gates continued service delivery during an appeal window. Absent on tasks unrelated to the continuation policy.

assignmentRoles.description: Five-role task-assignment projection (governance §7.2 + §10.2; workflow-governance.md:502-514). Surfaces `excludedOwner`/`requiredOwner`/`eligiblePool`/`currentAssignee`/`delegatedFrom`/`businessAdministrator` so a staff portal can render the full role table without re-deriving from delegation graphs. Absent when the task carries no role declarations beyond the top-level `assignedActor`/`delegatedFrom` fields (which mirror `currentAssignee`/`delegatedFrom` on this projection).

draftResponse.description: Most recent draft response saved via `POST /tasks/{id}/draft`. Present only when `status == drafted`.

draftedAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the draft was last saved. Present only when `status == drafted`.

lastValidationOutcome.description: Most recent validation outcome for the draft or submitted response.

createdAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the task was created.

updatedAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the task projection was last refreshed.

TaskListOptions.description: Query fields for `GET /api/v1/tasks` and `GET /api/v1/instances/{instanceId}/tasks` (ADR 0082 D-7). The default scope is the calling actor's inbox; admins MAY pass `assignedActor` to widen the scope subject to ReBAC checks.

assignedActor.description: Filter to tasks currently assigned to the supplied actor. Defaults to the calling actor.

status.description: Filter to tasks in the supplied posture. Repeating the query parameter narrows to a posture set.

kind.description: Filter to tasks of the supplied family.

instanceId.description: Filter to tasks on the supplied case instance. Implied (and required to match) when the request is the `/instances/{instanceId}/tasks` subresource.

workflowUrl.description: Filter to tasks on instances of the supplied Workflow Document.

deadlineBefore.description: Inclusive UTC RFC 3339 upper bound on `deadline`. Useful for surfacing tasks approaching SLA breach.

cursor.description: Opaque resume token returned by the previous page (ADR 0082 D-7).

limit.description: Maximum number of tasks requested for this page.

TaskPage.description: Cursor-paginated task page (ADR 0082 D-7). Items are `TaskListItem` projections for inbox-style consumption.

items.description: Task list items in this page.

cursor.description: Opaque token for the next page. Omitted when hasMore is false.

hasMore.description: True when another page is available from the server.

TaskDraft.description: Request body for `POST /api/v1/tasks/{id}/draft`. The endpoint saves a partial response without committing it; repeated drafts overwrite the prior draft. Idempotent on `(taskId, definitionUrl, definitionVersion, response)` — repeated identical drafts return the same drafted-state representation. The server runs the workflow's validation pipeline against the draft and returns the validation outcome on the resulting `Task` projection.

definitionUrl.description: Form or contract definition URL the draft was authored against. MUST match the task's pinned `definitionUrl`; mismatches are rejected with `WOS-1409`.

definitionVersion.description: Form or contract definition version. MUST match the task's pinned `definitionVersion`.

response.description: Partial response payload conforming to the task's contract.

draftedAt.description: Optional client-observed UTC RFC 3339 timestamp. The server MAY replace with its own receipt time.

TaskSubmission.description: Request body for `POST /api/v1/tasks/{id}/response`. The endpoint commits a final task response. The `Idempotency-Key` HTTP header is REQUIRED (ADR 0082 D-16). Successful submission transitions the task to `submitted`, fires the kernel `taskCompleted` event, and projects the response into case state via the workflow's `responseMappingRef`. Submission against a non-`pending`/`drafted` task is rejected with `WOS-1409`.

definitionUrl.description: Form or contract definition URL the response was authored against. MUST match the task's pinned `definitionUrl`.

definitionVersion.description: Form or contract definition version. MUST match the task's pinned `definitionVersion`.

response.description: Final response payload conforming to the task's contract.

submittedAt.description: Optional client-observed UTC RFC 3339 timestamp. The server MAY replace with its own receipt time.

signatureRef.description: Optional URN of a `SignatureAffirmation` provenance record produced by the signature profile. REQUIRED when `kind == signature`.

TaskDismissal.description: Request body for `POST /api/v1/tasks/{id}/dismiss`. The endpoint declines the task without submitting a response; the server transitions the task to `dismissed` and fires the kernel `taskSkipped` event with the supplied rationale. Dismissal against a non-`pending`/`drafted` task is rejected with `WOS-1409`. Idempotent on `(taskId, reason)`.

reason.description: Closed-with-vendor-extension dismissal rationale. Vendor extensions MUST use an `x-` prefix.

detail.description: Optional free-form rationale recorded in provenance.

dismissedAt.description: Optional client-observed UTC RFC 3339 timestamp. The server MAY replace with its own receipt time.

TaskOutcomeKind.description: Closed wire taxonomy for `TaskOutcomeEvent.outcome` (ADR 0082 D-12). Reserved literals: `submitted` (final response committed); `completed` (task response committed and case mutated); `dismissed` (actor declined); `expired` (SLA breach without submission); `delegated` (assignment changed via delegation); `escalated` (assignment escalated to a higher authority); `drafted` (a draft save event for downstream observers). No vendor-extension seam: these are the outcomes the public API surfaces to clients; richer kernel-side events live in provenance.

TaskOutcomeEvent.description: Wire shape of a task state change emitted to subscribed observers (notifications domain, server-side projections). Carries the task URN, the new posture, the actor responsible, and the outcome literal — enough for downstream projections to update without re-fetching the task. The structurally-identical `Task` resource is also retrievable; this event is the projection seam for incremental consumers.

instanceId.description: Case instance URN this task belongs to.

outcome.description: Closed outcome literal classifying this state change.

newStatus.description: Closed user-facing status the task entered as a result of this event.

actorRef.description: Actor URN responsible for this state change. Omitted only for system-driven outcomes (`expired`).

occurredAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the state change took effect server-side.

provenanceRecordRef.description: Optional URN of the provenance record that captures this outcome (Kernel S8). Present when the runtime has stamped the record before emission.
